Title: MdSINA2-MdNAC104 Module Regulates Apple Alkaline Resistance by Affecting γ-Aminobutyric Acid Synthesis and Transport

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis roots; apple calli; tomato Micro-Tom for heterologous test
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis roots; apple calli; heterologous tomato Micro-Tom also used
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves alkaline stress resistance; signaling metabolite
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GABA accumulation and efflux in roots and enhances alkaline resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdALMT13 is highly expressed in roots, induced by alkaline stress, and localized to the plasma membrane. 2. MdNAC104 directly binds the MdALMT13 promoter and represses its expression. 3. MdALMT13 transports GABA in yeast assays. 4. Overexpression of MdALMT13 increases root GABA accumulation and GABA efflux, increases root activity and growth, and decreases REL under alkaline stress; RNAi has opposite effects.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots and strongly induced by alkaline stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-high expression; 11.6-fold induction at 6 h alkaline stress
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkaline stress in hydroponics at pH 9 using NaHCO3:Na2CO3 = 1:1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; dual-LUC; GUS; Y1H; EMSA; yeast transport assay; overexpression/RNAi in roots; callus and tomato validation; GABA quantification in roots and hydroponic solution
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving alkaline tolerance by enhancing GABA retention and rhizosphere efflux; useful in rootstock engineering and stacking with MdGAD1/3 or MdSINA2
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions reported
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome under alkaline stress; accession NA
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ALMT proteins are often malate transporters, this study functionally supports MdALMT13 as a GABA transporter contributing to both root accumulation and efflux; overexpression also correlates with higher MdGAD1/3 transcript levels

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: GL-3 / Malus hupehensis system used for validation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis roots; GL-3; apple calli; heterologous tomato Micro-Tom also used
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves alkaline stress resistance in plants; signaling molecule and secondary metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for GABA synthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes GABA accumulation and enhances alkaline resistance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdGAD1 expression is induced by alkaline stress and is repressed by MdNAC104. 2. Overexpression of MdGAD1 in apple roots and calli increased GABA content, root activity/fresh weight, and reduced REL under alkaline stress, whereas RNAi caused the opposite. 3. Heterologous overexpression in tomato showed similar enhancement of alkaline resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots under alkaline stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; transcript increased after alkaline stress, notably at 3 h and 12 h
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkaline stress in hydroponics at pH 9 using NaHCO3:Na2CO3 = 1:1; exogenous GABA treatment used in module analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling (LC-MS GABA); transgenic roots/calli; heterologous expression in tomato; promoter repression by MdNAC104 via dual-LUC, GUS, Y1H, EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering alkaline-tolerant apple rootstocks or cultivars by increasing root GABA biosynthesis; can be stacked with MdGAD3, MdALMT13, or MdSINA2 and combined with repression/editing of MdNAC104
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome under alkaline stress; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdGAD1 was directly repressed by MdNAC104 through promoter binding; co-overexpression in MdNAC104-OE background rescued GABA level and stress tolerance

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis system used for root validation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M. hupehensis roots; apple calli/chimeric plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Improves alkaline stress resistance in plants; signaling molecule and secondary metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for GABA synthesis in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates alkaline resistance by increasing endogenous GABA content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdGAD3 is induced by alkaline stress and repressed by MdNAC104. 2. MdNAC104 directly binds the MdGAD3 promoter and suppresses its transcription. 3. Functional assays showed MdGAD3 has effects similar to MdGAD1, and its overexpression rescues the MdNAC104-overexpression phenotype under alkaline stress.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots under alkaline stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-expressed; markedly induced at early alkaline stress (3 h)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkaline stress in hydroponics at pH 9 using NaHCO3:Na2CO3 = 1:1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; promoter repression assays (dual-LUC, GUS, Y1H, EMSA); overexpression in roots/chimeric rescue; GABA quantification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for enhancing alkaline tolerance through increased GABA biosynthesis, particularly in combination with MdGAD1/MdALMT13/MdSINA2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions reported
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome under alkaline stress; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Detailed independent accession given; functional results mainly shown in supplementary data but directly integrated into the core module of the paper

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: GL-3 overexpression lines used; root RNAi in GL-3/Malus hupehensis systems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-response metabolite/signaling molecule improving alkaline resistance
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses MdGAD1, MdGAD3, and MdALMT13 by binding their promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator of root GABA homeostasis and alkaline resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces root GABA content and decreases alkaline resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdNAC104 expression is reduced under alkaline stress, and overexpression decreases root activity, increases REL, and lowers GABA content. 2. Exogenous GABA rescues the alkaline-sensitive phenotype of MdNAC104-overexpressing plants. 3. MdNAC104 directly binds and represses MdGAD1/3 and MdALMT13 promoters. 4. MdSINA2 ubiquitinates and degrades MdNAC104, relieving repression of the GABA pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in roots and downregulated under alkaline stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in roots under alkaline stress
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with MdSINA2 but no stable regulatory complex formally defined
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Alkaline stress; exogenous GABA supplementation; MG132 for proteasome inhibition in degradation assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi; exogenous GABA rescue; dual-LUC; GUS; Y1H; EMSA; Y2H; BiFC; Split-LUC; pull-down; Co-IP; ubiquitination and degradation assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing or suppressing MdNAC104 is a promising strategy to elevate root GABA and improve apple alkaline tolerance; can be paired with MdSINA2 activation or MdGAD1/3/MdALMT13 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression inhibits growth under alkaline stress; no major differences under normal conditions reported
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome under alkaline stress; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdNAC104 protein abundance declines under alkaline stress due to 26S proteasome-dependent degradation

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Stress-response metabolite/signaling molecule improving alkaline resistance
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SINA (Seven in Absentia) E3 ubiquitin ligase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Post-translational control of GABA biosynthesis/transport module via MdNAC104 stability
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ubiquitination and 26S proteasome degradation of MdNAC104, indirectly increasing MdGAD1, MdGAD3, and MdALMT13 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive upstream controller of root GABA accumulation under alkaline stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases root GABA content and improves alkaline resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdSINA2 is induced by alkaline stress and physically interacts with MdNAC104. 2. MdSINA2 ubiquitinates MdNAC104 and promotes its 26S proteasome-dependent degradation. 3. Overexpression of MdSINA2 increases root GABA content, root activity, and alkaline tolerance, while RNAi causes the opposite. 4. MdSINA2 overexpression in MdNAC104-OE background restores MdGAD1/3 and MdALMT13 expression and rescues stress tolerance.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated under alkaline stress; highest expression in roots among MdSINA genes mentioned
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in roots under alkaline stress
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through physical interaction with MdNAC104
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; Split-LUC; pull-down; Co-IP; in vitro and in vivo ubiquitination assays; cell-free degradation assay; overexpression/RNAi; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator for engineering alkaline tolerance by destabilizing MdNAC104 and derepressing the GABA module; suitable for gene stacking with MdGAD1/3 or MdALMT13
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious growth penalty under normal conditions reported
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Root transcriptome under alkaline stress; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts upstream of MdNAC104 and exerts indirect positive effects on GABA synthesis and efflux genes
